name: Fall Feast- Corn Chowder
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat oil in a pot over medium heat and add bacon. When the bacon has released its fat, add onion and potatoes and sauté for 2 minutes.
2. Add celery and green pepper and sauté another minute.
3. Add stock and bring to a boil. Add milk, thyme, and simmer together for 10 minutes or until vegetables are crisp-tender.
4. If using fresh corn: with a knife, slice corn kernels into the bowl and reserve liquid from the can. Add corn to the soup pot along with whipping cream, and simmer for 5 more minutes or until corn is tender.
5. Stir in parsley. Season well with salt, pepper, and hot pepper sauce as desired.
6. Serve with semi-dry Riesling wine.
Note: This recipe was published in Food & Drink magazine, Autumn 1994, by the Liquor Control Board of Ontario, Canada, written by Lucy Waverman.
